title: Privacy-Preserving Eye Tracking for Virtual Reality via On-Device Obfuscation
abstract: This paper studies privacy leakage from eye-tracking signals collected during virtual reality sessions and proposes a lightweight on-device obfuscation method.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Robot Navigation in Unknown Environments
abstract: We present a deep reinforcement learning approach that enables robots to navigate unknown environments using only raw sensor data without prior maps.

title: Efficient Transformer Architectures for Low-Resource Machine Translation
abstract: This work introduces a parameter-efficient transformer variant that achieves competitive translation quality with 40 percent fewer parameters on low-resource language pairs.

title: Detecting Fake News Using Graph Neural Networks and Source Credibility
abstract: We propose a graph neural network model that combines content analysis with source credibility signals to detect fake news articles across multiple domains.

title: Federated Learning for Healthcare: A Survey on Privacy Challenges and Solutions
abstract: This survey examines privacy-preserving techniques in federated learning for healthcare applications, analyzing trade-offs between model accuracy and patient data protection.

title: Self-Supervised Learning for Medical Image Segmentation with Limited Annotations
abstract: We develop a self-supervised pretraining method for medical image segmentation that reduces annotation requirements by 60 percent while maintaining diagnostic accuracy.

title: Blockchain-Based Supply Chain Transparency for Pharmaceutical Products
abstract: This paper proposes a blockchain framework for tracking pharmaceutical products through the supply chain, ensuring authenticity and preventing counterfeit drugs.

title: Adversarial Training Methods for Robust Natural Language Understanding
abstract: We investigate adversarial training techniques to improve the robustness of NLP models against synonym substitution and paraphrasing attacks.

title: Edge Computing Architectures for Real-Time IoT Data Analytics
abstract: This study designs an edge computing architecture that processes IoT sensor data in real-time, reducing latency by 75 percent compared to cloud-only solutions.

title: Multi-Modal Fusion for Emotion Recognition in Video Conversations
abstract: We present a multi-modal fusion approach combining facial expressions, speech patterns, and text transcripts for accurate emotion recognition in video conversations.

title: Neural Architecture Search for Efficient Convolutional Networks on Mobile Devices
abstract: This work develops a neural architecture search method that discovers efficient CNN models optimized for mobile device constraints without manual design.

title: Explainable AI for Credit Scoring: Interpreting Deep Learning Decisions
abstract: We propose an explainable AI framework for credit scoring that provides transparent explanations for deep learning model decisions while maintaining prediction accuracy.

title: Quantum Machine Learning Algorithms for Drug Discovery Applications
abstract: This paper explores quantum machine learning algorithms applied to molecular property prediction, demonstrating potential speedups for virtual drug screening.

title: Zero-Shot Text Classification Using Large Language Models with Prompt Engineering
abstract: We evaluate zero-shot text classification performance of large language models under various prompt engineering strategies across diverse text domains.

title: Cybersecurity Threat Detection Using Unsupervised Anomaly Detection on Network Traffic
abstract: This study develops an unsupervised anomaly detection system for identifying cybersecurity threats in network traffic without labeled attack examples.

title: Smart Agriculture: IoT Sensor Networks for Precision Irrigation Systems
abstract: We design an IoT sensor network for precision agriculture that optimizes irrigation schedules based on soil moisture, weather data, and crop water requirements.

title: Cross-Lingual Transfer Learning for Low-Resource Sentiment Analysis
abstract: This work investigates cross-lingual transfer methods that leverage high-resource language models to improve sentiment analysis in low-resource languages.

title: Meta-Learning for Few-Shot Image Classification in Medical Diagnostics
abstract: We propose a meta-learning approach that enables few-shot adaptation of image classifiers to rare medical conditions with minimal training examples.

title: Fairness and Bias Mitigation in Resume Screening Algorithms
abstract: This paper analyzes demographic biases in automated resume screening systems and proposes debiasing techniques that maintain predictive validity.

title: Optimizing Memory Usage in Large-Scale Graph Processing Systems
abstract: We present memory optimization techniques for distributed graph processing that reduce memory footprint by 50 percent while maintaining processing speed.

title: Speech Synthesis Using Diffusion Models for Natural-Sounding Voices
abstract: This work adapts diffusion probabilistic models to neural speech synthesis, generating more natural and expressive synthetic voices than autoregressive baselines.

title: Human Pose Estimation from Single Images Using Part Affinity Fields
abstract: We describe a real-time method for multi-person pose estimation using part affinity fields to associate body parts of individuals in the image.

title: Automated Code Generation from Natural Language Descriptions Using Transformers
abstract: This study develops a transformer-based model that generates executable Python code from natural language problem descriptions with 85 percent accuracy.

title: Privacy-Preserving Facial Recognition Using Homomorphic Encryption
abstract: We design a facial recognition system that performs matching on homomorphically encrypted biometric data, protecting user privacy throughout the authentication process.

title: Recommender Systems for Personalized Education: A Deep Learning Approach
abstract: This paper proposes a deep learning recommender system that personalizes educational content sequencing based on student knowledge states and learning objectives.

title: Detecting Depression from Social Media Text Using Attention Mechanisms
abstract: We develop an attention-based neural model that identifies depression indicators in social media posts, achieving early detection with 78 percent recall.

title: Energy-Efficient Deep Learning Inference on Resource-Constrained Microcontrollers
abstract: This work presents techniques for optimizing deep neural network inference on microcontrollers, achieving 10x energy reduction with minimal accuracy loss.

title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Vehicle Coordination
abstract: We propose a multi-agent reinforcement learning framework that enables autonomous vehicles to coordinate intersection navigation without centralized control.

title: Text Summarization with Controllable Length and Style Using Pointer-Generator Networks
abstract: This study extends pointer-generator networks with length and style control mechanisms for generating abstractive summaries with specified characteristics.

title: Vulnerability Detection in Smart Contracts Using Static Analysis and Machine Learning
abstract: We combine static analysis with machine learning to detect security vulnerabilities in Ethereum smart contracts before deployment on the blockchain.

title: 3D Object Detection from LiDAR Point Clouds Using Voxel-Based Neural Networks
abstract: This paper introduces a voxel-based neural network architecture for real-time 3D object detection from LiDAR point clouds in autonomous driving scenarios.

title: Counterfactual Explanations for Machine Learning Models in Lending Decisions
abstract: We generate counterfactual explanations for loan approval models, showing applicants what changes would lead to different lending decisions.

title: Transfer Learning for Wildlife Conservation: Automated Species Identification from Camera Traps
abstract: This work applies transfer learning to identify animal species from camera trap images, supporting biodiversity monitoring in protected areas.

title: Neural Machine Translation for Sign Language: Converting Video to Text
abstract: We develop a neural system that translates sign language videos into written text using spatial-temporal convolutional and recurrent networks.

title: Optimizing Database Query Performance Using Learned Index Structures
abstract: This study replaces traditional B-tree indexes with learned neural models, achieving up to 3x faster query times on sorted datasets.

title: Social Robot Navigation in Crowded Environments Using Deep Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We train social robots to navigate crowded human environments using deep reinforcement learning with human trajectory prediction.

title: Fake Review Detection on E-Commerce Platforms Using Behavioral Features
abstract: This paper identifies fake product reviews by analyzing reviewer behavioral patterns rather than review text content alone.

title: Compressed Sensing MRI Reconstruction Using Deep Learning for Faster Scans
abstract: We develop a deep learning method that reconstructs high-quality MRI images from highly undersampled k-space data, enabling faster patient scanning.

title: Knowledge Graph Completion Using Embeddings and Logical Rules
abstract: This work combines embedding-based methods with logical rule inference to complete missing facts in large-scale knowledge graphs.

title: Differential Privacy in Deep Learning: A Practical Implementation Guide
abstract: We provide practical guidelines for implementing differential privacy in deep learning training, with experiments on image classification benchmarks.

title: Handwritten Text Recognition Using Convolutional-Recurrent Neural Networks
abstract: This study develops a CNN-RNN hybrid model for recognizing handwritten text in historical documents without requiring character-level segmentation.

title: Active Learning Strategies for Minimizing Annotation Costs in NLP
abstract: We compare active learning strategies for NLP tasks, showing that uncertainty sampling can reduce annotation needs by 40 percent with minimal performance loss.

title: Deepfake Video Detection Using Temporal Inconsistency Analysis
abstract: This paper detects deepfake videos by analyzing temporal inconsistencies in facial movements across video frames using recurrent neural networks.

title: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback for Dialogue System Optimization
abstract: We train dialogue systems using reinforcement learning from human feedback, improving response quality and reducing harmful outputs.

title: Time Series Forecasting for Renewable Energy Production Using Neural Networks
abstract: This work develops neural network models for forecasting solar and wind energy production, improving grid stability through accurate predictions.

title: Automated Bug Localization in Software Using Deep Learning on Source Code
abstract: We propose a deep learning model that localizes software bugs by analyzing source code changes and bug reports without executing the program.

title: Cross-Modal Retrieval: Matching Images and Text Using Shared Embeddings
abstract: This study learns joint embedding spaces for images and text, enabling efficient cross-modal retrieval in large multimedia collections.

title: Data Augmentation Techniques for Small Medical Imaging Datasets
abstract: We evaluate data augmentation strategies for medical image classification when only limited training data is available from rare conditions.

title: Natural Language Inference Using Decomposable Attention Neural Networks
abstract: This paper presents an efficient neural architecture for natural language inference that decomposes the problem using attention mechanisms.

title: Sarcasm Detection in Online Conversations Using Contextual Embeddings
abstract: We develop a sarcasm detection model that uses conversational context and emoji patterns to identify ironic statements in social media.

title: Efficient Sparse Matrix Multiplication on GPUs for Graph Neural Networks
abstract: This work optimizes sparse matrix operations on GPUs to accelerate graph neural network training on large-scale graph datasets.

title: Domain Adaptation for Sentiment Classification Across Product Categories
abstract: We propose unsupervised domain adaptation methods that transfer sentiment classifiers between different product categories without labeled target data.

title: Autonomous Drone Path Planning Using Deep Q-Networks in GPS-Denied Environments
abstract: This study enables autonomous drone navigation in GPS-denied indoor environments using deep reinforcement learning with visual input only.

title: Named Entity Recognition for Scientific Literature Using SciBERT
abstract: We fine-tune SciBERT for named entity recognition in scientific papers, improving extraction accuracy of methods, materials, and metrics.

title: Anomaly Detection in Financial Transactions Using Autoencoder Neural Networks
abstract: This paper develops an autoencoder-based system for detecting anomalous financial transactions in real-time payment processing systems.

title: Multi-Task Learning for Joint Intent Detection and Slot Filling in Dialogue
abstract: We propose a multi-task learning framework that jointly optimizes intent detection and slot filling for task-oriented dialogue systems.

title: Text Style Transfer for Simplifying Medical Information for Patients
abstract: This work develops a text style transfer model that automatically simplifies complex medical literature for general patient comprehension.

title: Visual Question Answering for Blind Users: Describing Images from Natural Questions
abstract: We build a visual question answering system designed for blind users, providing natural language descriptions of images in response to spoken questions.

title: Optimizing Neural Network Compression Through Structured Pruning Techniques
abstract: This study evaluates structured pruning methods for neural network compression, finding optimal pruning ratios that balance speed and accuracy.

title: Predicting Protein-Protein Interactions Using Graph Convolutional Networks
abstract: We apply graph convolutional networks to predict protein-protein interactions from amino acid sequences and structural features.

title: Bias Detection in Word Embeddings: Measuring Gender Stereotypes
abstract: This paper quantifies gender bias in pre-trained word embeddings and proposes debiasing methods that preserve linguistic utility.

title: Scene Text Recognition in the Wild Using Attention-Based Sequence Models
abstract: We develop an attention-based sequence model that recognizes text in natural scene images with varying fonts, orientations, and lighting conditions.

title: Dialogue State Tracking for Multi-Domain Conversational Agents
abstract: This work improves dialogue state tracking across multiple domains using a slot attention mechanism that handles domain transitions.

title: Graph-Based Fraud Detection in Online Payment Networks
abstract: We model online payment networks as graphs and apply graph neural networks to detect fraudulent transactions and suspicious account patterns.

title: Automated Essay Scoring Using Regression-Based Neural Networks
abstract: This study develops neural regression models for automated essay scoring that achieve high agreement with human raters across diverse prompts.

title: Indoor Positioning Systems Using WiFi Fingerprinting and Deep Learning
abstract: We use deep learning on WiFi signal fingerprints to achieve room-level indoor positioning accuracy without dedicated hardware.

title: Language Model Pretraining on Scientific Text for Biomedical NLP Tasks
abstract: This work demonstrates that continued pretraining of language models on scientific literature improves performance on downstream biomedical NLP tasks.

title: Video Action Recognition Using SlowFast Networks for Surveillance Applications
abstract: We adapt SlowFast networks for real-time action recognition in surveillance video, detecting anomalous behaviors in public spaces.

title: Neural Rendering for Creating Photorealistic 3D Scenes from 2D Images
abstract: This paper presents a neural rendering approach that reconstructs photorealistic 3D scenes from sparse 2D photographs using implicit representations.

title: Spelling Correction for Search Engines Using Noisy Channel Models
abstract: We implement a noisy channel model for query spelling correction that handles context-dependent errors and out-of-vocabulary words.
